title: Bowie

jokes.text: Jemaine: Yes. Murray: Yes. Bret? Bret: No. Murray: What do you mean no? Bret: I'm not here. Murray: You're not here? Bret: No. Murray: Where are you? Bret: No, I was just joking.
jokes.setup: Standard roll call procedure
jokes.punchline: Bret claims he's not present while obviously being there
jokes.explanation: Classic Bret-style deadpan absurdity - perfectly in character and well-structured

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Bret?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: What do you mean no?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I'm not here.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You're not here?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Where are you?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No, I was just joking.

jokes.text: Murray explaining he's carried on band meetings without the band members being there
jokes.setup: Discussion about attendance at band meetings
jokes.punchline: Murray reveals he's held meetings for the band without the band
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray character moment - bureaucratic absurdity that could only come from him

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Murray explaining he's carried on band meetings without the band members being there

jokes.text: We're at a band meeting. We haven't been here? - Yeah, twice. I mean... - When? Well, it doesn't matter when. They didn't go very well.
jokes.setup: Murray claiming they've missed band meetings
jokes.punchline: The impossibility of missing meetings they're always at
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Murray's bureaucratic absurdity and the band's confused relationship with his management.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: We're at a band meeting. We haven't been here?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah, twice. I mean...

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: When?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, it doesn't matter when. They didn't go very well.

jokes.text: But I've had to circle where you two are.
jokes.setup: Murray showing what he thinks is a good band photo
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal that he had to circle the band members in the photo
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that effectively shows Murray's misguided management approach

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: But I've had to circle where you two are.

jokes.text: Photo of Gemma's 21st birthday with Murray circling random cousins thinking they're the band
jokes.setup: Murray trying to use existing photos for band promotion
jokes.punchline: The 'band photo' is actually strangers at a birthday party
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy that builds beautifully - multiple reveals make it funnier

dialogue.line: Photo of Gemma's 21st birthday with Murray circling random cousins thinking they're the band

jokes.text: Photo of Jemaine with ex-girlfriend Clea but with Bret's head superimposed
jokes.setup: Murray's attempts at creating band photos
jokes.punchline: Bizarre photoshopped romantic photo with inappropriate head replacement
jokes.explanation: Deeply uncomfortable and hilariously specific visual gag that's perfectly absurd

dialogue.line: Photo of Jemaine with ex-girlfriend Clea but with Bret's head superimposed

jokes.text: I made it. I made this one. You can't take my personal photos and stick other people's heads over... I loved that photo.
jokes.setup: Bret admitting he made the weird photo
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's hurt about his romantic photo being defaced
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing the band's dysfunction and Jemaine's sensitivity.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I made it. I made this one.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You can't take my personal photos and stick other people's heads over... I loved that photo.

jokes.text: Murray: 'this one here, you're not in. And this one here you're in, but you shouldn't be'
jokes.setup: Explaining the photo problems
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact summary of the absurd photo situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd logic - economical and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: 'this one here, you're not in. And this one here you're in, but you shouldn't be'

jokes.text: So those are you options. This one here, you're not in. And this one here you're in, but you shouldn't be.
jokes.setup: Murray summarizing the photo options
jokes.punchline: Both options are terrible in opposite ways
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan summary with excellent economy. Classic Murray logic.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: So those are you options. This one here, you're not in. And this one here you're in, but you shouldn't be.

jokes.text: Murray refusing to let them look through passport camera because it's 'New Zealand government property'
jokes.setup: Photo shoot with professional equipment
jokes.punchline: Overly bureaucratic protection of a simple camera
jokes.explanation: Classic Murray bureaucratic mindset applied absurdly

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You can't look through that. It's New Zealand government property.

jokes.text: Murray: 'Who wants to hear a sad band?' Jemaine: 'This is the cool look for a band.' Murray: 'But what about your hands in the air and smile?' Jemaine: 'That's gymnasts, Murray.'
jokes.setup: Disagreement about proper band photo poses
jokes.punchline: Murray confusing band photos with gymnastics
jokes.explanation: Murray's cultural confusion played perfectly straight

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: 'Who wants to hear a sad band?'

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: 'This is the cool look for a band.'

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: 'But what about your hands in the air and smile?'

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: 'That's gymnasts, Murray.'

jokes.text: That's gymnasts, Murray.
jokes.setup: Murray suggesting bands put their hands in the air and smile
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's correction that Murray is thinking of gymnasts
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan correction that highlights Murray's cluelessness about music

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: That's gymnasts, Murray.

jokes.text: Jemaine: 'you look like an ogre that works in the library'
jokes.setup: Critiquing the photo results
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and creative insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and unexpected insult - highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: you look like an ogre that works in the library

jokes.text: Sometimes I'm glad that we don't live in New Zealand anymore. 'Cause of all those penguins. - Remember how there'd be penguins?
jokes.setup: Random conversation about penguins
jokes.punchline: Acting like penguins were a major daily nuisance in New Zealand
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor about New Zealand. Fits their characters but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Sometimes I'm glad that we don't live in New Zealand anymore. 'Cause of all those penguins.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Remember how there'd be penguins?

jokes.text: Yeah... no.
jokes.setup: Jemaine asking if Bret likes penguins
jokes.punchline: Bret's confused/contradictory response
jokes.explanation: Simple but characteristic Bret response showing his uncertainty

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Yeah... no.

jokes.text: Jemaine repeatedly asking if Bret is bulimic when Bret just wants to eat more
jokes.setup: Bret explains he feels small and needs to eat more
jokes.punchline: Jemaine misinterprets everything as bulimia
jokes.explanation: Running gag that builds through repetition and misunderstanding

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I feel small. I need to eat more.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Are you bulimic?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No, I just need to eat more food.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Are you bulimic?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Are you bulimic?

jokes.text: Jemaine claiming he heard Bret being 'a little bit bulimic' in the bathroom after his mom's birthday
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bret's supposed eating disorder
jokes.punchline: Treating being drunk and throwing up as 'being bulimic'
jokes.explanation: Escalates the bulimia running gag with specific absurd example

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I heard Bret being a little bit bulimic in the bathroom after his mom's birthday

jokes.text: Bret asking for compliments and Jemaine refusing because 'it'd be weird'
jokes.setup: Bret's vulnerability about needing validation
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's immediate rejection of showing emotional support
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - their emotional awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Can you just tell me I'm good at something?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, it'd be weird.

jokes.text: Jemaine dressed as David Bowie claiming 'It's 1972 David Bowie from the Ziggy Stardust tour'
jokes.setup: Bret wakes up to find someone in his room
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's elaborate Bowie costume and character commitment
jokes.explanation: Unexpected visual gag that establishes the dream sequence premise

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: It's 1972 David Bowie from the Ziggy Stardust tour

jokes.text: Bret: 'Wow, you look a lot like Jemaine' Jemaine: 'No, I'm David Bowie'
jokes.setup: Obvious disguise pretending to be someone else
jokes.punchline: Bret's matter-of-fact observation and Jemaine's denial
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that acknowledges the absurdity while staying in character

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Wow, you look a lot like Jemaine

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, I'm David Bowie

jokes.text: Bret's guinea pig and giraffe dream descriptions
jokes.setup: Discussion about freaky dreams
jokes.punchline: Mundane animal transformations described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Typically Bret non-sequitur that derails the conversation

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Bret's guinea pig and giraffe dream descriptions

jokes.text: Yeah, okay. Listen, Bret, I don't have much time, man.
jokes.setup: Bret rambling about his dreams
jokes.punchline: Dream Bowie cutting him off impatiently
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat to Bret's rambling. Efficient character work.

dialogue.speaker: Bowie
dialogue.line: Yeah, okay. Listen, Bret, I don't have much time, man.

jokes.text: Bowie's advice: 'Get an eye patch, man' and Bret already having one
jokes.setup: Mysterious advice from dream Bowie
jokes.punchline: Bret coincidentally already owns the suggested accessory
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed reveal that makes the advice both prophetic and mundane

dialogue.speaker: Bowie
dialogue.line: Get an eye patch, man

jokes.text: Murray describing them as 'two different sizes there. There's the full range.'
jokes.setup: Murray promoting the band to someone
jokes.punchline: Describing the band's height difference as a 'full range'
jokes.explanation: Murray's bizarre promotional spin on an obvious physical fact

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: two different sizes there. There's the full range.

jokes.text: No no, he's not a midget. He's just... he's thin.
jokes.setup: Murray on phone describing the band
jokes.punchline: Correcting misconception about Bret's size
jokes.explanation: Murray's awkward phone manner. Functional but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: No no, he's not a midget. He's just... he's thin.

jokes.text: Greeting cards with hidden music technology - Murray's confused explanations
jokes.setup: Explaining the greeting card business opportunity
jokes.punchline: Murray's complete inability to understand simple technology
jokes.explanation: Murray's technological confusion played as character trait

dialogue.line: Greeting cards with hidden music technology - Murray's confused explanations

jokes.text: It's a company that sells greeting cards. - Greeting cards? - But you know those ones that open up and they have a tune in them?
jokes.setup: Murray building up a big meeting
jokes.punchline: It's just greeting cards, not a record deal
jokes.explanation: Classic deflation of expectations. Murray's overhype vs. reality.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: It's a company that sells greeting cards.
Greeting cards?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: But you know those ones that open up and they have a tune in them?

jokes.text: Jemaine asking the woman to flirt with Bret because 'I think it might be gay' to compliment him himself
jokes.setup: Trying to help Bret's confidence
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's fear of appearing gay prevents basic friendship
jokes.explanation: Escalates the earlier compliment refusal with added homophobia anxiety

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I think it might be gay to compliment him myself

jokes.text: Mel's elaborate description of gay activities: 'Erotically caressing each other on the hood of a car... in the back of a movie theater'
jokes.setup: Explaining what would actually be gay
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and bizarre sexual scenarios
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation from helpful explanation to vivid scenarios

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Erotically caressing each other on the hood of a car... in the back of a movie theater

jokes.text: Doug was fired as senior professor but still hangs around the university
jokes.setup: Asking what Doug does at the university
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of being fired but refusing to leave
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect casualness

dialogue.speaker: Doug
dialogue.line: Doug was fired as senior professor but still hangs around the university

jokes.text: Jemaine's awkward compliments: 'Your beard is good' and 'You're good at finding shortcuts'
jokes.setup: Attempting to give Bret compliments
jokes.punchline: Extremely weak and specific non-compliments
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of awkward male friendship - Jemaine's discomfort palpable

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Your beard is good

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You're good at finding shortcuts

jokes.text: Your beard is good. It's just a compliment for you... your beard. Just a compliment about your beard being good.
jokes.setup: Jemaine trying to give Bret compliments
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-explanation of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Jemaine's social awkwardness in giving compliments. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Your beard is good. It's just a compliment for you... your beard. Just a compliment about your beard being good.

jokes.text: 'Bret, you got it going on' song - the entire musical number
jokes.setup: Jemaine decides to write a song to cheer up Bret
jokes.punchline: Song becomes increasingly homoerotic despite constant denials
jokes.explanation: Masterpiece of musical comedy - escalates from innocent to deeply uncomfortable while maintaining character logic

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Bret, you got it going on

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You're like a beautiful woman

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You're like a beautiful woman

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: And I want to make love to you

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: But it's not gay

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I'm just saying you're beautiful

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Like a man can say to another man

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: He's beautiful

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: And it's not weird

jokes.text: 'Not in a gay way, just in a hey, man, I wanted to say / That you're looking okay, man, why can't a heterosexual guy / Tell a heterosexual guy that he thinks his booty is fly?'
jokes.setup: Song attempting to avoid gay implications
jokes.punchline: Immediately sexualizes with 'booty is fly' while claiming heterosexuality
jokes.explanation: Peak moment of the song - perfectly constructed ironic contradiction that became a cultural touchstone

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Not in a gay way, just in a hey, man, I wanted to say / That you're looking okay, man, why can't a heterosexual guy / Tell a heterosexual guy that he thinks his booty is fly?

jokes.text: 'I put a wig on you when you were sleeping / I put a wig on you... you-hoo-hoo-hoo / And I just lay there and spooned you'
jokes.setup: Song supposedly about friendship and confidence
jokes.punchline: Reveals actual sexual fantasy enacted while Bret slept
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that crosses every line while remaining musically beautiful - comedy gold

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I put a wig on you when you were sleeping / I put a wig on you... you-hoo-hoo-hoo / And I just lay there and spooned you

jokes.text: Jemaine denying he said anything about wigs: 'I think sometimes you hear what you want to hear'
jokes.setup: Bret questioning the wig lyrics
jokes.punchline: Gaslighting about something we just heard him sing
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that maintains Jemaine's denial despite obvious evidence

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I think sometimes you hear what you want to hear

jokes.text: 'It wouldn't be gay to put a wig on a man and pretend they're a woman. How could that be gay if you were pretending they're a woman?'
jokes.setup: Defending the wig action
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic that makes it sound worse
jokes.explanation: Jemaine's warped logic making the situation even more disturbing

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: It wouldn't be gay to put a wig on a man and pretend they're a woman. How could that be gay if you were pretending they're a woman?

jokes.text: Not that I did it.
jokes.setup: Jemaine explaining why the wig thing wouldn't be gay
jokes.punchline: Weak attempt to backtrack from his implicit admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic timing with minimal dialogue maximum impact

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Not that I did it.

jokes.text: 1980 David Bowie confirming that putting a wig on a sleeping friend isn't gay
jokes.setup: Bret asking dream Bowie about the wig situation

jokes.explanation: Absurd validation from authority figure in Bret's subconscious

dialogue.line: 1980 David Bowie confirming that putting a wig on a sleeping friend isn't gay

dialogue.speaker: Bowie
dialogue.line: Hey, it's 1980 David Bowie, from the music video 'Ashes to Ashes.'

jokes.text: Actually, come to think of it, the same thing happened to me when I wore an eye patch. It was... oh, it was always, 'Bowie's bumped into a door.'

jokes.punchline: Imagining paparazzi coverage of Bowie's clumsiness
jokes.explanation: Funny headline and humanizes the rock star. Good absurdist touch.

dialogue.speaker: Bowie
dialogue.line: Actually, come to think of it, the same thing happened to me when I wore an eye patch. It was... oh, it was always, 'Bowie's bumped into a door.'

jokes.text: Do something absolutely outrageous. - Like what? - Well, you'll know what to do, Bret. And you'll know exactly when the time is right.
jokes.setup: Bowie giving vague advice
jokes.punchline: Cryptic non-advice that sets up future disaster
jokes.explanation: Sets up the penis reveal disaster. Good foreshadowing as comedy.

dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine/Bowie
dialogue.line: Well, you'll know what to do, Bret. And you'll know exactly when the time is right.

jokes.text: Well, you're looking good today, Bret. Very hot. - Thank you. - Hotter than Jemaine.

jokes.punchline: Going overboard and comparing to Jemaine

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Well, you're looking good today, Bret. Very hot. Hotter than Jemaine.

dialogue.line: Thank you.

jokes.explanation: Mel's inappropriate over-analysis. Gets specific in funny way.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: You have a refined bone structure whereas Jemaine's facial features are too deep-set to be classically handsome.

dialogue.line: Like headphones? No, this is a computer chip... Like a Walkman. No it's not... not like a Walkman at all.

jokes.text: 'Bowie's in Space' song - entire musical number

jokes.text: 'Do you want to borrow my jumper, Bowie? / Does the space cold make your nipples go pointy, Bowie?'
jokes.setup: Practical concerns about space travel
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and childlike questions about space physics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret-style innocent questions that become unexpectedly sexual

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Do you want to borrow my jumper, Bowie? / Does the space cold make your nipples go pointy, Bowie?

jokes.text: 'Do you use your pointy nipples / As telescopic antennae to transmit data back to Earth?'
jokes.setup: Pointy nipples question

jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that turns innocent question into absurd sci-fi concept

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Do you use your pointy nipples / As telescopic antennae to transmit data back to Earth?

jokes.text: 'Do you have one really funky sequined space suit / Or do you have several ch-changes?'

jokes.punchline: Pun on Bowie's song 'Changes' incorporated naturally
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that references Bowie's catalog while maintaining song narrative

dialogue.line: Do you have one really funky sequined space suit / Or do you have several ch-changes?

jokes.text: 'Do you smoke grass out in space, Bowie? / Or do they smoke... / AstroTurf?'
jokes.setup: Question about drug use in space
jokes.punchline: Pun substituting artificial grass for marijuana
jokes.explanation: Solid pun with good setup and payoff timing

dialogue.line: Do you smoke grass out in space, Bowie?
Or do they smoke...
AstroTurf?

dialogue.line: I'm picking it up on my L.S.D. Screen

jokes.punchline: Bret's simple takeaway about not worrying about confidence

jokes.text: Is it a really big biscuit? - It's not a biscuit! I said no biscuit. It's not gonna be another kind, is it?

jokes.punchline: Murray's paranoid accounting over tiny amounts
jokes.explanation: Murray's business paranoia over microscopic amounts. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Can I get a copy of that, Murray?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: No no, 'cause that would cost more than 50¢, and then we'd... we'd be down, wouldn't we?

jokes.text: Final reprise of 'Bowie's in Space' with vocal flourishes
jokes.setup: Episode ending
jokes.punchline: Returning to the space song as musical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Solid musical conclusion that reinforces the episode's signature song

dialogue.line: Final reprise of 'Bowie's in Space' with vocal flourishes

tmdb_overview: After a photo session, Bret develops body image issues and gets some dream advice from his idol, David Bowie. Jemaine plots to cheer him up and Murray tries to get one of the band's tunes used for a musical greeting card.
